# UI5 Web Components for React

> React component library wrapping SAP's UI5 Web Components for building enterprise-grade, Fiori-compliant applications.

This MCP server provides documentation and API assistance for consumers of UI5 Web Components for React packages.

## Scope

This server covers these public packages:
- `@ui5/webcomponents-react` — React wrappers for UI5 Web Components + custom React components
- `@ui5/webcomponents-react-charts` — Chart components built on recharts
- `@ui5/webcomponents-ai-react` — AI-enhanced component wrappers
- `@ui5/webcomponents-react-base` — Public utilities: Device API, ThemingParameters, hooks

This server does NOT cover:
- Build tooling, bundler configuration, or deployment
- `@ui5/webcomponents-react-compat` (deprecated/legacy components)
- `@ui5/webcomponents-react-cli` (internal tooling)
- Internal/private APIs of `@ui5/webcomponents-react-base`
- The UI5 Web Components themselves (only their React wrappers)

## Available MCP Tools

- `create_app`: Scaffold a new application from an official template (Vite, Next.js). Use when the user wants to create a new project.
- `list_components`: Browse all available components by category or package. Use this first to discover component names.
- `get_component_api`: Get detailed props, methods, and type information for any component. Use after list_components.
- `get_documentation`: Read documentation guides, search across topics, and retrieve full content with line-range support.
- `get_public_utils`: Get documentation for Device API, ThemingParameters, useI18nBundle, useViewportRange, and withWebComponent from @ui5/webcomponents-react-base.

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](docs--Welcome.mdx): Installation, peer dependencies, ThemeProvider configuration, React 18+ setup, wrapper components concept
- [Project Templates & Examples](docs--ProjectTemplates.mdx): Vite, CRA, Next.js, and CodeSandbox project templates with pre-configured setups

### Knowledge Base

- [Bring Your Own Web Components](docs--knowledge-base--BringYourOwnWebComponents.mdx): Wrapping custom web components with CLI tools and code-mods
- [Common CSS](docs--knowledge-base--CommonCSS.mdx): CSS Modules, design tokens, theming parameters, CSS variables
- [FAQ](docs--knowledge-base--FAQ.mdx): Frequently asked questions, troubleshooting, best practices
- [Handling Slots](docs--knowledge-base--CustomComponentsSlots.mdx): Web Components slot mechanism, named slots, content projection
- [IDs via Dataset](docs--knowledge-base--ChangeEvents.mdx): HTML data-* attributes, dataset API, DOM element identification
- [Internationalization](docs--knowledge-base--Internationalization.mdx): Multi-language support, locale configuration, RTL, number/date formatting
- [Public Utils](docs--knowledge-base--Public-Utils.mdx): Utility functions from @ui5/webcomponents-react-base, ThemingParameters
- [Server Side Rendering](docs--knowledge-base--ServerSideRendering.mdx): SSR with Next.js, shadow DOM challenges, hydration strategies
- [Styling](docs--knowledge-base--Styling.mdx): CSS variables, shadow parts (::part), custom states (:state), ThemingParameters, CSS bundles
- [Accessibility](upstream--accessibility.mdx): Accessibility APIs, proxy attributes, label-input relationships, screen reader support

## Key Concepts

- Web components emit CustomEvents with data in `e.detail` (not `e.value`)
- For controlled components, call `e.preventDefault()` to prevent internal state updates
- Slot props require forwarding the `slot` prop to the root element
- Shadow DOM styling uses `::part()` pseudo-element and CSS variables (`--sapBackgroundColor`, etc.)
- Components support both React 18 and React 19+
- Use subpath imports for @ui5/webcomponents-react-base (e.g., `@ui5/webcomponents-react-base/hooks`)
- Web components use proxy attributes (`accessibleName`, `accessibleNameRef`) instead of standard `aria-*` for shadow DOM compatibility
- When creating a new application, prefer official project templates (Next.js, Vite) over scaffolding from scratch. Use create_app to scaffold from a template. Only fall back to generic framework setup if no template matches the user's framework.
